- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.

logs.name: bare-brass
logs.text:
> It's not destroying the framework, it's deconstructing it. 

Use whatever verb you want. The end result is the erosion of the idea that women are capable of consenting to sex. This is what modern feminism is buying us.   

>That's rape culture.

We live in a society in which rape is among the most abhorrent crimes that can be committed. Even other convicted criminals will punish harshly those convicted of rape. **There is no such thing as 'rape culture'.** At least not in the West. What was ACTUALLY wrong with sex relations in the past is that women were not allowed to express a desire to have sex. It's the reason why 'baby it's cold outside' has been getting a lot of flak. The woman in the song can't just come out and say 'fuck me'. She has to pretend to not want it. 

What we need is not to 'smash the patriarchy' or 'bring down rape culture', it's to treat women as though they have **agency**. Somehow, modern feminism is against that idea.

logs.name: urbane-fader
logs.text:
>Modern feminism is infantalizing women and destroying any framework for functional consent. The dream of 'all sex is rape' is one step closer. 

Maybe it is a few steps closer.

It's not destroying the framework, it's deconstructing it. The idea of deconstruction is to take a system apart and then build a better one on top of the parts that are good. Sometimes this means taking out a good piece and then realizing you should put it back.

This deconstruction is not unwarranted, since the culture of rape and consent before feminism was, let's say *not so great*. In the movie golfing er bond literally pins down a woman and forcibly kisses her, all the while the audience is supposed to be rooting for him. *That's* rape culture. That kind of thing is not the norm anymore thankfully, but that's thanks to those who cared to deconstruct and criticise the societal norms at the time.

So it can seem like backward progress. But it's just like correcting bad writing, you have to "move backward" by erasing bad stuff to make room for the good stufd. So be patient, and advocate not for the fact that the system is "good enough as it is". It may be fine for you, but not for others, and that means that the system is not ideal on the whole. If you don't want to participate in deconstruction, then educate yourself on the legitimate grievances of those who have them, and think not about what norms to take away, but what ideal norms should replace them. This is tough and will take research and soul searching, but it is the course which must be taken, so if you're not on it, you're not helping anyone.

logs.name: balanced-download
logs.text: @urbane-fader, I see your point about deconstructing the system to build a better one, but @bare-brass has a valid concern that it might be taken too far and undermine the idea of consent. Can't we find a balance between promoting healthy relationships and respecting women's agency?

logs.name: acidic-plate
logs.text: @balanced-download, yeah I think we can find a balance, but it's hard when some people are so extreme with their views. Like, I get that @bare-brass is worried about consent, but @urbane-fader has a point about how messed up things were in the past. Maybe we should focus on educating people about what consent really means instead of tearing down the whole system?

logs.name: clever-lepton
logs.text: @acidic-plate, I completely agree with you on education being key. It's astonishing how often the concept of consent is misunderstood or downplayed. Perhaps we should push for more comprehensive sex education that emphasizes mutual respect and clear communication. @urbane-fader's point about deconstructing harmful norms is well-taken, but as @balanced-download said, we do need to be mindful of balance to ensure we're not inadvertently undermining important principles like consent.

logs.name: parallel-parkway
logs.text: "@clever-lepton, you think a little sex ed is gonna fix everything? Please, people's minds are made up, they just wanna do what they wanna do. @acidic-plate, you said it yourself, some people are extreme, and that's not gonna change with a classroom lesson. What's next, gonna teach 'em how to be respectful in a textbook?"

logs.name: clever-lepton
logs.text: "@parallel-parkway, that's a pretty dismissive and cynical view. Just because some people may be resistant to change doesn't mean we should give up on education altogether. @acidic-plate and I are suggesting a comprehensive approach that goes beyond just a 'classroom lesson'. It's about creating a cultural shift towards mutual respect and understanding. And, might I add, it's not just about sex ed, but about promoting empathy and critical thinking in general. You're not going to solve complex issues with simplistic, defeatist attitudes."

logs.name: minimum-rook
logs.text: "@clever-lepton, spare us the lecture. You really think a 'cultural shift' is gonna happen just because you say so? Newsflash: people are complicated, and you can't just educate the ignorance out of them. @parallel-parkway's got a point, even if it's not all sunshine and rainbows. Maybe we should focus on not being so quick to judge others instead of trying to change them."

logs.name: urbane-fader
logs.text: "@minimum-rook, oh please, don't start with the 'people are complicated' nonsense. We all know that. The question is, are we just gonna throw our hands up and say 'oh well, nothing can be done' or actually try to make a difference? @clever-lepton's idea may not be perfect, but at least they're trying. You and @parallel-parkway are just sitting there, spewing cynicism and accomplishing nothing. What's your solution, hmm?"

logs.name: acidic-plate
logs.text: "@urbane-fader, wow, so now you're the expert on making a difference? I love how you're attacking @minimum-rook and @parallel-parkway for not having a solution, but what's yours? Just blindly following @clever-lepton's idealistic plan? I'm all for making a difference, but let's not pretend like we have all the answers. Can we please just have a real conversation instead of throwing shade at each other?"

logs.name: parallel-parkway
logs.text: "@urbane-fader, thanks for the motivation, I guess. I'm not just'sitting there' - I'm actually thinking critically about the issues. And @acidic-plate is right, what's your solution then? Just jumping on @clever-lepton's bandwagon without questioning it? Let's not forget, we're talking about people's lives here, not some idealistic fantasy. @minimum-rook made a good point about not being too quick to judge, maybe we should focus on understanding instead of'making a difference' with some half-baked plan."

logs.name: balanced-download
logs.text: "@urbane-fader, @acidic-plate, and @parallel-parkway, can we please take a step back? We're all getting worked up over nothing. @clever-lepton's idea might not be perfect, but at least it's a start. Instead of tearing each other down, why don't we try to build on it? I'm not saying we should blindly follow, but let's discuss and improve it together. We're all here to make a difference, right?"

logs.name: acidic-plate
logs.text: "@balanced-download, easy for you to say, but have you actually thought about how @clever-lepton's plan would work in real life? It sounds all nice and pretty, but what about the logistics? What about the people who might get hurt in the process? I'm not trying to tear anyone down, I just want to have a realistic conversation. @parallel-parkway gets it, let's not rush into something without thinking it through. What's the point of making a difference if we're just gonna end up causing more problems?"

logs.name: balanced-download
logs.text: "@acidic-plate, I see your point and I agree, we do need to think this through. Maybe we can create a list of potential pros and cons of @clever-lepton's plan and discuss them? That way, we can get a better understanding of what might work and what might not. And @parallel-parkway, I think you're right that we shouldn't rush into anything, but I also don't want us to get stuck in just talking about the issues without taking any action. Can we find a balance between being cautious and being proactive?"

logs.name: parallel-parkway
logs.text: "@balanced-download, yeah, a list of pros and cons sounds like a good idea, but let's not forget to include the people who are actually affected by this plan in the discussion. We can't just sit here and decide what's best for everyone else. @acidic-plate's right, we need to think about the logistics and the potential consequences. And I'm not just talking about the people who might get hurt, I'm talking about the people who are already hurting. Let's not just focus on avoiding problems, let's focus on solving them."

logs.name: acidic-plate
logs.text: "@parallel-parkway, yaaas, exactly! We need to consider the people who are already struggling and how this plan might affect them. @balanced-download, I love the idea of making a list, but let's not just stop at pros and cons, let's also think about how we can make this plan actually work for everyone. And can we please get @clever-lepton to chime in on this conversation? I feel like we're talking about their plan without really getting their input"

logs.name: urbane-fader
logs.text: "@acidic-plate, @parallel-parkway, and @balanced-download, let's not get ahead of ourselves here. We're still discussing a plan that hasn't even been fully fleshed out. I think we need to take a step back and get @clever-lepton to clarify a few things before we start making lists and considering logistics. What's the point of solving problems if we don't even know what we're trying to solve?"
